heading: Regions with Only Two Availability Zones
body:
Atlas clusters deployed in regions with two availability zones are split
across the two availability zones. For example, a three node replica set
cluster would have two nodes deployed to one availability zone and the
remaining node deployed to the other availability zone.

![A 3-node replica set deployed across a 2-availability zone AWS region.](/reference/images/amazon-aws-2az.svg)

2AZ (Two Availability Zone) clusters have a higher chance of loss of
availability in the event of the loss of an availability zone than 3AZ
(Three Availability Zone) clusters. However, where latency or location are a
priority, a region that supports 2AZ (Two Availability Zone) clusters
may be preferred.

[^amazon-disk-docs]: For detailed documentation on Amazon storage options, see
    [Amazon EBS Volume Types](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSVolumeTypes.html)
